title: Neotinea ustulata
taxonomy: Neotinea ustulata is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Neotinea, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. More specifically, it is placed within the subtribe Orchidinae, which includes a variety of orchid species. The classification of Neotinea ustulata is further refined to the order Asparagales, reflecting its evolutionary relationships with other plants. This classification system is widely used in botany to categorize and organize plant species.
uses: Neotinea ustulata, also known as the burnt orchid, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its roots and rhizomes are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The plant is also said to have astringent properties, making it useful for treating digestive issues and diarrhea. In some cultures, Neotinea ustulata is used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant.

intro: Neotinea ustulata, also known as the burnt orchid, is a rare and endangered species of terrestrial orchid native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that may aid in the treatment of various health issues, such as fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Neotinea ustulata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the burnt orchid is used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, although its use is largely limited due to its rarity and potential side effects.
